title: Police, Punk, and Crosswords: An Interview with Tony White
description: I think that's a reality of policing. Not just undercover policing. The job requires a kind of professional mask, a professional, and procedural carapace that an experienced police officer will adjust according to the situation. They’re well trained in such procedures. And I wanted to reflect on that kind of life. To come back to Javier Cercas’ idea of the ‘blind spot’ briefly, undercover police, whatever they were doing, were putting themselves in positions of extreme risk: risk of discovery, v
hook: A cop explains the professional masks he wore undercover—and what happens when the performance becomes indistinguishable from the person.

title: Naomi Kanakia has two questions about n+1: How did it used to publish so much consistently amazing work? Why did it get so much worse?
description: Naomi Kanakia has two questions about n+1: How did it used to publish so much consistently amazing work? Why did it get so much worse?
hook: Why did n+1 go from publishing consistently brilliant work to something unrecognizable? A reckoning with a magazine's inexplicable decline.

title: Trouble Was by Charlotte Edwardes review – a sharp child’s-eye view of adult neglect
description:
A young boy and his two siblings stay with their aunt in the West Country, in this haunting debut set over the long, hot summer of 1976

The summer of 1976 calls to my generation of novelists. We don’t remember it, but we remember the textures of daily life in that era, and a heatwave puts daily life under the kind of pressure that fuels fiction. In Guardian journalist Charlotte Edwardes’s first novel, Trouble Was, the scene is set by that heatwave with its attendant, escalating water sh
hook: A child's summer in 1976 England becomes a study of adult negligence—heat, boredom, and what happens when no one's watching.

title: The Prom Went On in Kyiv, but Masha’s Date Danced Alone
description: "Masha Polska, 15, was an avid dancer who had been dreaming of a star turn in the group waltz. That was not to be."
hook: A 15-year-old dancer's prom in Kyiv during the war. Her date showed up. She couldn't.

title: Root and Branch: An Interview with Nuhara
description: Sicilian trio Nuhara have produced one of the best albums of the year in their debut, Rama. They speak to Patrick Clarke about how the discovery that a member's grandfather had been recorded by Alan Lomax led to a deep dive into the island's traditional worksongs, from which they've drawn a record of ferocious power and remarkable sonic sc
hook: Sicilian trio Nuhara made one of the year's best albums by accident. They explain how discovery works when you're not looking for it.
